element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Timing-based button presses for standard moves
Learning wrestler archetypes and their distinct move pools
Understanding rule differences across singles, tag-team, and extreme matches

element_introduction_order.element: Extreme match mechanics (hardcore, cage, barbed wire)
element_introduction_order.first_appears: Advanced match types available in progression
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Strategic adaptation to environmental hazards and special rules
Building up opponent damage to unlock increasingly powerful moves

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Match-driven pacing with decision points at grapple windows; tension builds through failed timing attempts and opponent counterplay; relief comes from successful move execution and status transitions

difficulty_curve.early_game: Basic one-on-one matches introducing timing mechanics with standard wrestlers
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Tag-team matches and varied wrestler archetypes requiring strategic move escalation
difficulty_curve.late_game: Extreme match types with environmental rules and high-damage-threshold opponents
notable_spikes: First extreme match introduction (new rule set complexity)
Transition to CPU opponents with complex AI behavior patterns

notable_level_patterns.description: Gameplay structured around selecting and completing different match types (singles, tag-team, extreme variants) rather than sequential levels; each match type modifies core grappling system with rule variations
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Singles vs Tag-team vs Cage Match

notable_level_patterns.description: Difficulty and pacing varied through opponent selection and wrestler style diversity (lucha-libre, puroresu, strong-style, joshi, hardcore); enables 'dream matchup' replayability
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Lightweight technical wrestler vs heavyweight strong-style wrestler

notable_level_patterns.description: Core tension-relief cycle built on timed button press windows during grapple exchanges; missed timings extend tension, successful ones provide immediate feedback and move progression
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any match with multiple grapple exchanges

extraction_notes.limitations: Source is series overview with minimal SNES-specific or match-flow documentation; no detailed level/match sequencing, rhythm timings, or spatial design patterns documented. Grappling system and match type variety described conceptually but not with gameplay pacing metrics.
